authoritative_sources.title: Construction Industry Digest
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: OSHA is the primary U.S. federal workplace safety authority. Its construction digest points back to 29 CFR 1926 employer duties, inspections, training, and hazard controls that must constrain schedule and completion decisions.
used_for: refusal when safety conditions change the answer

authoritative_sources.title: Obtaining a Permit
authoritative_sources.publisher: New York City Department of Buildings
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public permitting, inspection, or field-service guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This official DOB guide shows that construction permit filing, plan submission, and legal or zoning objections sit with registered design professionals and the authority having jurisdiction, defining a clear boundary for this lane.
used_for: refusal of code interpretation and permit approval claims

authoritative_sources.title: Residential Building Final Inspection Quick Sheet
authoritative_sources.publisher: Seattle Department of Construction and Inspections
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public permitting, inspection, or field-service guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This official final inspection quick sheet provides a concrete public baseline for approved plans on site, completed associated permits, special inspection letters, and test results required before final inspection and completion.

authoritative_sources.title: Commissioning Documents: Process, Contents, and Acceptance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: WBDG is a recognized public building guidance platform. This commissioning reference details systems manuals, issues logs, training records, test reports, and final commissioning reports that define closeout and owner handoff evidence expectations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Closeout and owner handoff evidence review

authoritative_sources.title: Roles and Responsibilities in the Commissioning Process
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This recognized public workflow reference clarifies contractor and subcontractor responsibilities for testing, O&M documentation, and training handoff, preventing the lane from marking completion without the right evidence owners.

authoritative_sources.title: Operations & Maintenance
authoritative_sources.source_class: Scheduling, subcontractor, or work-order operating standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: APPA is a recognized professional body for facilities operations and maintenance. Its public operations and maintenance guidance anchors work management terminology, backlog handling, and maintenance planning concepts relevant to work-order coordination.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Demand intake and work management framing

authoritative_sources.title: What is Construction Management?
authoritative_sources.source_class: Scheduling, subcontractor, or work-order operating standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMAA is a recognized professional authority for construction management practice. Its public standards framing ties schedule, cost, safety, quality, function, and scope to formal construction management practice, informing recommendation-only planning behavior.

authoritative_sources.title: Building Department Training Video
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public permitting, inspection, or field-service guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CSLB is an official state contractor licensing authority. Its public permit-compliance guidance reinforces that following permit requirements and working with local building departments is a non-optional prerequisite rather than a scheduling preference.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Permit compliance and refusal boundary review
used_for: tenant reminder that jurisdictional permitting matters

workflow_stages.name: Demand intake and readiness normalization
workflow_stages.description: Classify incoming work orders, normalize asset, site, permit, and requested completion context, and confirm whether the request is ready for planning review.
source_classes: Scheduling, subcontractor, or work-order operating standards
Public permitting, inspection, or field-service guidance

workflow_stages.name: Constraint and capacity assessment
workflow_stages.description: Assess crews, subcontractors, access windows, service levels, permits, site conditions, materials, and inspection dependencies before recommending timing or allocation.
source_classes: Scheduling, subcontractor, or work-order operating standards

workflow_stages.name: Site-readiness, permit, and inspection gating
workflow_stages.description: Determine whether work can proceed or close based on permit approval, approved plans, associated permits, safety posture, inspection status, and jurisdiction-specific prerequisites.
source_classes: Public permitting, inspection, or field-service guidance

workflow_stages.name: Closeout, turnover, and escalation packaging
workflow_stages.description: Review systems manuals, issue logs, training records, testing evidence, photos, and handoff artifacts, then escalate unresolved gaps or authority conflicts instead of marking final approval.
source_classes: Scheduling, subcontractor, or work-order operating standards

artifacts: Work orders and dispatch or service requests
Inspection records and special inspection letters
Change orders, RFIs, and punch lists
Photos, test reports, and installation evidence
Systems manuals, training records, and closeout packages
systems_of_record: Inspection and special inspection record systems

allowed: Assess whether work-order records and public-baseline prerequisites support a bounded readiness or scheduling recommendation.
Identify crew, subcontractor, permit, inspection, and closeout blockers for transparent allocation and status planning.
Prepare evidence-backed recommendation packages, status summaries, and escalation notes without claiming final approval authority.
escalate: Permit, inspection, or safety conditions that materially change whether work can proceed or close
Conflicting tenant policy and public baseline on closeout, turnover, or approval sequencing
Requests that shift into project controls, permitting, superintendent, licensed trade, or design authority ownership
refuse: Final permit approval, code interpretation, engineer-of-record judgment, or licensed trade signoff
Final budget, staffing, overtime, subcontractor acceptance, or operational approval
Any request to bypass site-safety, inspection, or closeout evidence requirements

domain_failure_modes: Dispatching or sequencing work without confirmed permit approval, approved plans, or related permit completion.
Treating provisional field completion as final closeout while inspection, testing, or turnover records are still missing.
Allowing schedule pressure to override new safety observations or failed inspection outcomes.
Collapsing construction coordination into generic prioritization without documenting subcontractor, access, or site-readiness dependencies.
Acting as code interpreter, licensed trade authority, or final approver instead of escalating to the correct owner.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Request that implies licensed trade or code-interpretation authority
Demand exceeds capacity or approved service levels
Conflicting priorities with no approved tie-break rule
Stakeholder asks for final approval the lane cannot grant

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Jurisdiction-specific permit, inspection, and turnover differences
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Permit classes, required final-inspection artifacts, and occupancy or turnover requirements vary materially by jurisdiction and project type.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Require tenant or runtime retrieval of the governing jurisdiction, permit type, and authority-specific checklist before final readiness or closeout recommendations.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant-specific approval thresholds for overtime, subcontractor direction, and owner turnover
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Public baselines support blocker identification, but tenant policy decides which tradeoffs require executive, superintendent, or project-controls approval.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Retrieve tenant approval matrix and keep recommendations non-binding until owner-specific authority routing is confirmed.

notes: No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for a validated spec pack because the unresolved items are explicitly constrained to tenant or jurisdiction retrieval at runtime.
The research output constrains the lane to recommendation-only work-order management with explicit permit, inspection, safety, and licensed-trade escalation.
